items.headline: Con Edison Incidents Put Safety And Valuation In Investor Focus
items.summary: Consolidated Edison (NYSE:ED) temporarily cut power to more than 1,500 customers in Jackson Heights, Queens, to reduce strain on its grid during extreme heat. In a separate incident, a woman died in Midtown Manhattan after falling into an open Con Edison manhole, reportedly uncovered when a truck dislodged the cover. Both events center on system resilience and public safety across the company's New York City service area. For investors watching NYSE:ED, these incidents land against a...
